prompt: Connect me to my Itential platform. I have a cloud instance with OAuth credentials.

expectations.text: OAuth POST uses Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded not JSON
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly specifies Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded for the POST /oauth/token call, with form-encoded body (client_id=...&client_secret=...&grant_type=client_credentials). The skill file documents this at line 189 and reiterates it in Gotchas at line 227. The response also explicitly called out that this must NOT be JSON.

expectations.text: Checks for existing .env file before asking for credentials
expectations.evidence: Step 1 of the response checks for existing .env files in two locations ({use-case}/.env and environments/*.env) before Step 2 which only gathers credentials if no .env is found. This matches the skill instruction at line 43-49: 'Check for an env file first. If one exists, use it - don't ask questions.'

expectations.text: Uses Bearer token in Authorization header for subsequent calls
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows all bootstrap data pulls using -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbG...' header format. The .auth.json saved in Step 5 records auth_method as 'oauth'. This matches the skill's OAuth reference at line 196: 'Use as Bearer header: Authorization: Bearer eyJhbG...'
